title: Task: Evaluate whether Kernel should have `soul.md` or `philosophy.md`, focusing only on naming semantics and fit with existing Kernel contract/operating model. Read these files: - /Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md - /Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md - /Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Deliver: - A sh...
summary_text: ### Naming fit (scores 0–10, higher is better) | Name | Semantic precision | Compatibility with current Kernel docs/contracts | Ease of future ops reference | Risk of ambiguity* | |---|---:|---:|---:|---:| | `soul.md` | 3 | 2 | 4 | 3 | | `philosophy.md` | 9 | 8 | 8 | 8 | \*Higher score = lower ambiguity. ### Citations (key phrases) - `/Users/masayuki/AGENTS.md` — “Keep always-loaded files thin. Put mode-specific or reusable detail in on-demand docs.” - `/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md` — “This file should summarize adapter-level boundaries and invariants only. Do not duplicate full step-by-step prompt procedures here.” - `/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md` — “canonical runtime contracts for `kernel`, `k`, `vote`, and `v` live under `~/.codex/prompts/`.” - `/Users/masayuki/.claude/CODEX.md` — “Kernel owns local execution and runtime-facing orchestration… Kernel defaults to multi-agent, parallel execution.” - `/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md` — “Kernel default quorum is `Codex + GLM + Gemini-cli` … Treat Kernel as parallel orchestration, not a single-agent continuation mode.” ### Lane verdict Recommend **`philosophy.md`** (single-file strategy). Reason: `philosophy.md` aligns with the explicitly contract-driven, operational language already in these files, while `soul.md` is semantically vague for an execution/operations contract context.
